titleInfo.title: Zhoushi yu jingchuang──9 shiji beike suojian fojiao sengsu chinian de tuoluoni
咒石與經幢──9世紀碑刻所見佛教僧俗持念的陀羅尼
Spell Stones and Sutra Pillars—Stone Carved Dhāranīs of the Ninth Century Memorized by Buddhist Clerics and Laypeople

mods.abstract: Liu Shufen mainly writes about two examples: (1) A "spell stone" as part of the tomb stupa 窣堵波塔 of monk Lingcan 靈璨, who made his own tomb pagoda in 828 while still alive. The inscription lists 12 short dharanis. Epigraphical works do not mention it; Beijing tushuguan holds a rubbing (BJTSG 30: 8). (2) An octagonal sutra pillar 經幢 erected in 870 in Tianning Si 天寧寺, Huzhou 湖州 in Zhejiang by Zhao Kuangfu 趙匡符. carved on the first six sides is the sutra text of T#967 (佛頂尊勝陀羅尼經 Uṣṇīṣavijayādhāraṇī Dhāraṇī Sutra of the Buddha’s Jubilant Corona), yet in a particular version, which adds another section to the 54 sections found in Taisho’s #967. 14 dharanis follow on the remaining two sides of the pillar. An inscription on the pillar enumerates which sutras and dharanis were recited by the donor Zhao Kuangfu how many times. Exactly these dharanis were carved on the pillar.
